MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST BUILDING PRODUCTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Oren Noah of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; G. Randy Kasten of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP (398.0200) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Gregory Janiszewski's deposition testimony and declaration, Mr. Luchetti's deposition testimony and Mr. Hamilton's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether Mr. Alex Janiszewski was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Hamilton red dot all-purpose joint compound) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Gregory Janiszewski's deposition testimony and declaration are overruled. Defendant's moving papers failed to make an issue of substantial factor causation. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of Aiken Welch, CSR #6099 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES.
Appearances: Anne T. Acuna of Brayton Purcell (415-898-1555) for plaintiff and responding party; Erica J. Saxum of DeHay Elliston LLP (510-285-0748) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed. The court will rule on plaintiff and defendant's objections once counsel re-submit a courtesy copy of objections with a proposed order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of Aiken Welch, CSR #12434 (503/TLJ)
